response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with strong web, backend, and open-source credentials, but their profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project), with the last decade dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web/platform work. There is no evidence of IEC 62443 / SDL secure-engineering practice, MQTT or industrial protocols, or industrial automation domain exposure, all of which are core to this position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source maintainership
Event-driven architecture and distributed-platform experience via the GISCollective geo-data platform on Kubernetes
concerns: Very limited and outdated professional C++/STL experience relative to a role centered on hands-on C++ development
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure, which the role treats as core competencies
No MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or IoT/industrial-automation domain background

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and systems-performance background
